outputs.title: Graduate futures intelligence operating model
outputs.description: The whole-deliverable operating model that brings together the data specification, caveat handling, Power BI V1/V2 route and pack production model for programme-level graduate futures intelligence.
acceptanceCriteria: Data specification, caveats, taxonomy and comparator principles are coherent across the product route.
Power BI V1 and V2 development have a clear ownership and maintenance model.
The pack model can be used across programme, department or faculty contexts where data allows.

outputs.title: Programme workshop and sense-making model
outputs.description: The whole-deliverable model for turning insight packs into structured programme conversations, including workshop structure, facilitator guidance, wayfinder validation and priority rollout principles.
acceptanceCriteria: Workshop materials guide users from data interpretation to action and articulation.
Wayfinder testing has validated the framing, usability and outputs of the approach.
Priority rollout principles and facilitator requirements are agreed.

outputs.title: Programme output set
outputs.description: The repeatable output set that programmes, departments or clusters produce through the process: Graduate Premium Profiles, Graduate Futures Action Plans, Story Pack material and structured returns.
acceptanceCriteria: Priority segments produce agreed profiles, action plans, story material and structured returns by September 2027.
The remaining agreed coverage population produces equivalent outputs where appropriate during the wider rollout.
Outputs distinguish evidence, interpretation, action and student-facing story material.

outputs.title: Data refresh and BAU ownership model
outputs.description: A sustainable model for refreshing the data, maintaining Power BI, updating packs and connecting refreshed intelligence to planning cycles.
acceptanceCriteria: Refresh cadence and owners are confirmed.
Power BI maintenance and pack update routes are agreed.
Refreshed data has a route into programme, faculty and institutional planning.

outputs.title: Institutional graduate futures synthesis and feed-through
outputs.description: Institutional synthesis of destination patterns, strategy types, strengths, support needs, priority segment learning and infrastructure needs, with a route into related King's Edge work.
acceptanceCriteria: Faculty returns are synthesised into patterns and support needs.
Findings feed related King's Edge deliverables and curriculum enhancement activity.
The synthesis distinguishes evidence, interpretation and recommended institutional action.

measures.title: Data specification readiness
measures.questionAnswered: Has the data specification been completed in time for Power BI V1?
measures.measure: Completion of specification, inventory, caveat register, taxonomy and comparator framework.
measures.target: Data specification and analytical framework complete by September 2026.

measures.title: Power BI V1 validation
measures.questionAnswered: Has Power BI V1 been built and validated strongly enough to inform V2?
measures.measure: UAT findings covering clarity, relevance, interpretation, caveats and usefulness.
measures.target: Power BI V1 tested with selected users by September 2026.

measures.title: V2 pack dissemination and showcase
measures.questionAnswered: Have V2 packs been produced, disseminated and showcased?
measures.measure: Completion of V2 packs, dissemination plan and evidence of institutional access and visibility.
measures.target: V2 packs disseminated and showcased by Christmas 2026.

measures.title: Initial institutional reporting
measures.questionAnswered: Has the first data deep dive become faculty discussion and Education Committee reporting?
measures.measure: Faculty roundtables completed and executive report submitted.
measures.target: Faculty roundtables held and Education Committee report submitted by Christmas 2026.

measures.title: Workshop model validation
measures.questionAnswered: Have wayfinder programmes validated the workshop approach?
measures.measure: Wayfinder workshop completion and feedback on usability, credibility, framing, actionability and outputs.
measures.target: Wayfinder programmes complete meaningful tests by Christmas 2026.

measures.title: Responsible interpretation
measures.questionAnswered: Are users handling caveats, comparators and marginal destinations appropriately?
measures.measure: Use of caveat guidance, validation feedback, wayfinder feedback and review of returns.
measures.target: Caveat guidance is used consistently in packs and workshop materials.

measures.title: Priority segment rollout
measures.questionAnswered: Has the refined model reached agreed priority segments by September 2027?
measures.measure: Completed priority workshops, structured returns, profiles, action plans and story materials.
measures.target: Priority segment workshops completed by September 2027.

measures.title: Action orientation
measures.questionAnswered: Does the process lead to realistic actions with owners and support needs?
measures.measure: Quality and completeness of Graduate Futures Action Plans.
measures.target: Completed action plans for programmes included in each rollout wave.

measures.title: Data refresh and BAU integration
measures.questionAnswered: Has the product moved into a BAU refresh and maintenance model?
measures.measure: Confirmed refresh cadence, owner, Power BI maintenance route and pack update process.
measures.target: BAU ownership model confirmed before full rollout completes.

measures.title: Remaining rollout and synthesis
measures.questionAnswered: Has the workshop model reached remaining coverage and produced synthesis?
measures.measure: Completion of final rollout wave and synthesis into patterns, strengths and support needs.
measures.target: Remaining rollout and fuller synthesis completed during 2027/28.

resources.fundingSummary: Whole-route resourcing depends on protected data product capacity, delivery coordination, faculty facilitator time and a sustainable BAU ownership model. Step-level resources describe where each type of capacity is needed in the delivery route.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own or co-own the graduate futures data product, interpretation principles, caveat guidance and BAU refresh route.
Lead the pack, workshop and rollout product route across the deliverable.
Provide the faculty decision and delivery route for validation, priority rollout and wider coverage.
Own benefit realisation and follow up agreed programme actions through normal department and faculty routes.
Maintain institutional graduate futures coherence and lead the synthesis into related King's Edge work.

newInvestment.item: Protected analytical and Power BI build capacity
newInvestment.rationale: Needed to produce, validate and maintain a credible Power BI and pack model rather than relying on ad hoc manual production.
Could support pack preparation, workshop logistics, return tracking, evidence handling and institutional synthesis across rollout waves.

enablingConditions.condition: Sustained access to graduate outcomes, LEO, TEF, benchmarking, enrolment supplement and relevant internal datasets.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The model cannot provide a credible programme-level graduate futures view.

enablingConditions.condition: Power BI prioritisation or equivalent scalable data visualisation route.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The insight pack may remain static or manually produced, limiting institutional use.

enablingConditions.condition: Faculty agreement to own local facilitation rather than expecting central delivery for every programme.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The central team becomes the bottleneck and coverage is not achievable.

enablingConditions.condition: Heads of Department accept benefit ownership for programme actions.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Action plans are produced but not owned or followed up.

enablingConditions.condition: Programme confirmation is accepted as sufficient approval for Story Pack use.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: Story Pack activation becomes too slow or over-governed.

investmentAsk.priority: High for Power BI V1/V2 delivery, validation and rollout coordination
investmentAsk.rationale: The deliverable is likely to need protected analytical, Power BI and coordination capacity. Step-level plans identify where the capacity is used; the deliverable-level ask is the overall resourcing decision.

risks.title: The work becomes too centrally resource-intensive
risks.description: Central teams cannot facilitate programme conversations for every programme or manually sustain all pack production.
risks.mitigation: Use the faculty-owned train-the-trainer model, with central support focused on data, method, templates, training and synthesis.

risks.title: Data is over-interpreted or used punitively
risks.description: Small numbers, suppression, volatility, coding issues or comparator misuse could lead to unfair or unhelpful interpretations.
risks.mitigation: Use caveat guidance, facilitator training, destination taxonomy and responsible interpretation principles throughout the product and workshop route.

risks.title: Faculty rollout is inconsistent
risks.description: Cascaded delivery may vary in quality, tone and completion across faculties.
risks.mitigation: Use a common toolkit, Vice Dean decision route, facilitator training, structured returns and progress tracking.

risks.title: Action plans are not followed up
risks.description: Programme action plans may be created but not owned or monitored after the workshop.
risks.mitigation: Confirm Heads of Department as benefit owners and route follow-up through internal department and faculty planning processes.

risks.title: Data product does not move into a sustainable BAU model
risks.description: Power BI, data refresh and pack maintenance could remain dependent on project goodwill after rollout.
risks.mitigation: Confirm refresh cadence, product ownership and maintenance route before full rollout completes.

issues.title: Protected data product and coordination resource needs confirmation
issues.description: The deliverable requires a whole-route decision about protected analytical, Power BI and coordination capacity.

issues.title: Faculty-owned delivery route needs to be locked
issues.description: The train-the-trainer model depends on Vice Deans Education or delegates confirming local facilitation expectations and benefit ownership routes.

issues.title: BAU refresh and synthesis route needs confirmation
issues.description: The data product, annual refresh and institutional synthesis need a confirmed ownership and reporting route before the project becomes business as usual.
issues.owner: Careers and Employability Service and Mark Peace

assumptions.title: Faculty-led delivery is necessary for scale
assumptions.summary: The central team cannot deliver programme workshops for every programme, so faculties must facilitate through a train-the-trainer model.

assumptions.title: Heads of Department can own benefit realisation
assumptions.summary: Programme actions will only create value if Heads of Department own follow-up through normal department and faculty planning routes.

assumptions.title: Programme confirmation is enough for Story Pack use
assumptions.summary: Programme teams can confirm that Story Pack language is accurate, fair and evidence-informed before local use.

assumptions.title: The data product can become a maintained service
assumptions.summary: The deliverable assumes that Power BI maintenance, data refresh and pack updates can move from project delivery into a sustainable BAU route.

decisions.title: Confirm protected resource for data product and coordination
decisions.description: Decide whether additional analyst, Power BI or coordination capacity is required, including whether a sandwich year placement or equivalent support should be pursued.
decisions.decisionMaker: Vice Deans Education or delegates, with Careers and Employability Service

decisions.title: Confirm faculty-owned delivery and benefit ownership route
decisions.description: Agree that the train-the-trainer model is the route to scale, and confirm how Heads of Department own action follow-up after programme conversations.

decisions.title: Confirm BAU data refresh and product ownership model
decisions.description: Agree how Power BI, data refresh, pack updates and access routes will be owned after the initial delivery phase.
decisions.decisionMaker: Careers and Employability Service with Vice Deans Education or delegates

decisions.title: Confirm institutional synthesis and feed-through route
decisions.description: Agree where the institutional typology and synthesis will be reviewed and how findings feed related King's Edge deliverables.
decisions.decisionMaker: Mark Peace and Vice Deans Education or delegates
